stories.title: PERKESO LINDUNG Scheme Rebranding — Update All System Labels from SKSPS/SKSSR to LINDUNG Family Names
stories.description: On August 22, 2025, PERKESO officially rebranded all four protection schemes under the unified 'LINDUNG' umbrella: SKSPS (Self-Employed Social Security) → LINDUNG KENDIRI; SOCSO employee scheme → LINDUNG PEKERJA; EIS (Employment Insurance System) → LINDUNG KERJAYA; SKSSR (Housewives Social Security) → LINDUNG KASIH. All payroll system UI labels, report headers, form descriptions, fixture field options, and statutory correspondence that reference the old scheme names must be updated. This is not a cosmetic change — official PERKESO communications, contribution portals, and ASSIST Portal workflows now exclusively use LINDUNG branding, and mismatched labels create regulatory confusion for HR staff and auditors.
acceptanceCriteria: All UI labels, page titles, and report headers using 'SKSPS' are updated to 'LINDUNG KENDIRI'
All UI labels using 'SOCSO scheme' in the LINDUNG PEKERJA context are updated to 'LINDUNG PEKERJA'
All UI labels using 'EIS' in contribution context are annotated with 'LINDUNG KERJAYA (EIS)' where the old acronym must be retained for regulatory form compliance
Existing Gig Workers Act stories' UI references updated from 'SKSPS' to 'LINDUNG KENDIRI' in all user-visible strings
Internal code identifiers (field names, doctype names) retain old names for backward compatibility; only user-visible strings are updated
A mapping table is maintained in code comments: SKSPS=LINDUNG KENDIRI, SKSSR=LINDUNG KASIH, SOCSO=LINDUNG PEKERJA, EIS=LINDUNG KERJAYA
technicalNotes: PERKESO official rebranding announcement: August 22, 2025
The LINDUNG branding covers four schemes; only LINDUNG KENDIRI (gig/self-employed) and LINDUNG PEKERJA (employees) are directly relevant to payroll
Statutory forms (Borang 8A, Borang 34, etc.) still use 'SOCSO'/'PERKESO' — do not change those form names
PERKESO ASSIST Portal login URL unchanged: https://assist.perkeso.gov.my/
Gig Workers existing stories reference 'SKSPS' — coordinate with all three Gig Workers Act stories to update labels
dependencies: Gig Workers Act 2025 — Auto-Register Gig Workers Under PERKESO SKSPS via ASSIST Portal API
Gig Workers Act 2025 — SKSPS Per-Transaction Contribution Calculation and PERKESO Monthly Aggregate Remittance

stories.title: Budget 2026 — Double Tax Deduction for Hiring Prisoners Released on Licence and Drug Dependents (YA2026–YA2030)
stories.description: Malaysia's Budget 2026 expanded the existing further deduction (double deduction) incentive for employers who hire vulnerable persons to include two new categories effective from YA2026 to YA2030: (1) prisoners released on licence under the Prisons Act 1995, and (2) drug/substance dependents and misusers currently undergoing approved treatment or rehabilitation programmes. Previously, the double deduction applied only to OKU (persons with disabilities), senior citizens, single parents, and ex-convicts under the Income Tax Act. The payroll/HR system must flag employees under these new categories, collect the required supporting documentation references, and include them in the annual double deduction schedule submitted as supporting evidence for the employer's tax return.
acceptanceCriteria: Employee DocType gains two new custom fields: custom_prisoner_licence_status (Check, 'Prisoner Released on Licence under Prisons Act 1995') and custom_drug_rehab_participant (Check, 'Drug/Substance Dependent in Approved Rehabilitation')
Each flag field has a corresponding document reference field: custom_prisoner_licence_doc_ref (Data) and custom_drug_rehab_doc_ref (Data) for storing the approval or certification reference number
YA eligibility guard: flags can only be set for employees hired from YA2026 onward; a validation warning fires if set for employees with hire_date before 2026-01-01

The annual Double Deduction Schedule report (generated before employer tax filing) includes employees in all eligible categories: OKU + Senior Citizen + Single Parent + Ex-Convict + Prisoner on Licence + Drug Rehab Participant
Report groups employees by category with total annual remuneration paid — this is the double deduction base amount for the employer's income tax claim
A warning is triggered if either new flag is set without a corresponding document reference being populated
technicalNotes: Income Tax Act 1967 Schedule 4B (further deductions for employment of vulnerable persons); Budget 2026 gazette expected early 2026
Supporting documents: letter from Prisons Department (for Prisoners Released on Licence); letter from Ministry of Health / Welfare Department (for drug rehabilitation participants)
The deduction is on the employer's corporate tax return, not on the employee's PCB — this is an employer-side tax benefit, not an employee relief
Existing OKU double deduction tracking story: 'Track OKU (Disabled Employee) Kad OKU Status and Generate Double Deduction Annual Schedule' — extend the same module
Budget 2026 key highlights: https://www.crowe.com/my/-/media/crowe/firms/asia-pacific/my/crowemy/news/key-highlights-of-malaysias-budget-2026--for-individuals-and-businesses-newsletter.pdf
dependencies: Track OKU (Disabled Employee) Kad OKU Status and Generate Double Deduction Annual Schedule

stories.title: MySIP 1:3 Internship Ratio Compliance Dashboard for Employers with Employment Pass Holders
stories.description: TalentCorp's 1:3 Internship Policy (effective April 1, 2026 after pilot extension to March 31, 2026) requires employers holding Employment Passes for foreign nationals to offer MySIP structured internship placements to local students proportionally: 1 intern per EP Category I holder, 2 interns per EP Category II holder, and 3 interns per EP Category III holder, capped at 2% of total Malaysian workforce. Non-compliance will adversely affect future EP renewal applications — a critical operational risk. The existing MySIP story ('MySIP Structured Internship Stipend Minimum Threshold Validation') covers stipend amounts and tax deductions but does not track the ratio compliance logic against actual EP headcount. This story addresses the missing ratio tracking dimension.
acceptanceCriteria: Employee DocType gains a custom_ep_internship_ratio_category Select field (None / EP Category I / EP Category II / EP Category III) for non-Malaysian EP holders
System computes monthly minimum MySIP internship obligation = (EP Cat I count × 1) + (EP Cat II count × 2) + (EP Cat III count × 3), capped at floor(total_malaysian_headcount × 0.02)

Active MySIP Intern count is tracked via a new MySIP Intern record linked to the Employer, with fields: intern_name, nric_or_student_id, institution, ep_holder_linked (Link to Employee), start_date, end_date, weekly_stipend
A MySIP Compliance Dashboard shows: required internship slots, active intern count, compliance status (Compliant / Shortfall / Not Applicable), and next EP renewal date
Alert fires 60 days before any EP holder's renewal date if the employer is in Shortfall status
A compliance flag is added to the EP holder's employee record: custom_internship_obligation_met (Yes / No / Not Applicable)
technicalNotes: Policy ratios: EP Category I → 1:1; EP Category II → 1:2; EP Category III → 1:3 (i.e., 1 EP holder requires X interns)
2% workforce cap: if computed slots > 2% of Malaysian employee headcount, cap applies
Pilot phase extended to March 31, 2026; mandatory enforcement from April 1, 2026
Minimum stipend: RM600/month for degree-level interns, RM500/month for diploma-level interns
TalentCorp MySIP portal: https://www.talentcorp.com.my/mysip-incentives/
Fragomen policy guide: https://www.fragomen.com/insights/malaysia-new-internship-policy-to-require-employers-with-employment-pass-employees-to-hire-interns.html
dependencies: MySIP Structured Internship Stipend Minimum Threshold Validation and Double Tax Deduction Payroll Tagging
Employment Pass Minimum Salary Threshold Validator (June 2026 Revised EP Policy)

stories.title: EPF Foreign Worker Permanent Departure Withdrawal Advisory Checklist on Employment Termination
stories.description: With mandatory EPF contributions for foreign workers effective October 2025 (2% employer + 2% employee), employers now accumulate EPF savings on behalf of non-Malaysian employees. When a foreign employee's employment terminates, resigns, or their work pass expires, they are eligible to withdraw their full EPF balance under the 'Leaving Country' provision. However, the application must be initiated at least 2 months before departure. If the employer fails to inform the employee of this entitlement, the foreign worker may forfeit time-sensitive withdrawal rights. The payroll system must auto-generate a structured withdrawal advisory checklist on termination of any non-Malaysian employee with EPF contributions.
acceptanceCriteria: On Employee record status change to 'Left' / 'Terminated' / 'Resigned' for a non-Malaysian employee (nationality != 'Malaysian'), system auto-creates an 'EPF Departure Withdrawal Advisory' task assigned to HR Manager
Advisory task includes: employee name, total EPF balance (if available), termination date, and a 60-day countdown from termination date to departure-assumed-date

Advisory checklist items: (1) Obtain work pass cancellation from Immigration, (2) Provide resignation/termination letter to employee, (3) Obtain income tax clearance (CP21), (4) Employee to apply via KWSP i-Akaun or KWSP office 2 months before departure
Task includes a link to the official KWSP Leaving Country withdrawal page

If the employee has no EPF contributions (joined before October 2025 mandatory date or is a domestic servant exclusion), advisory is still created but with a note: 'No mandatory EPF contributions — check if voluntary contributions exist'
Task closure requires HR to record: advisory_delivered_date and employee_acknowledged (Check)
technicalNotes: KWSP Act 1991 Section 54 — Leaving Country withdrawal provision
Application timeline: 2 months before departure; processing takes several weeks
Required documents: cancelled work pass OR termination letter, income tax clearance, bank account details
Domestic servants (maids, cooks, cleaners, drivers) are excluded from mandatory EPF contributions — flag via existing 'domestic_servant_exclusion' field
Coordinate with existing CP21/e-SPC workflow for tax clearance step
KWSP official Leaving Country guide: https://www.kwsp.gov.my/en/member/account-centre/leaving-country
dependencies: Auto-Submit CP21/CP22A/CP22B via e-SPC MyTax at Cessation or Departure
Foreign Worker EPF Oct 2025 Mandate: Domestic Servant Category Exclusion Flag

stories.title: e-Duti Setem Per-Employee Contract Stamp Duty Compliance Task with 30-Day Countdown (Effective January 2026)
stories.description: From January 1, 2026, all employment contracts must be stamped within 30 days of signing via LHDN's new e-Duti Setem system (accessible through the MyTax Portal). A fixed stamp duty of RM10 applies per employment contract under Item 4, First Schedule of the Stamp Act 1949. Late stamping incurs escalating penalties. Contracts executed before January 1, 2025 are exempt. The existing story 'Track Employment Contract Stamp Duty Compliance via e-Duti Setem MyTax (Mandatory from 2026)' establishes the general framework but does not specify per-employee task auto-creation with countdown. This story implements the operational per-hire task generation and reference number recording.
acceptanceCriteria: On new Employee record creation with a hire_date of 2026-01-01 or later, system auto-creates a 'Contract Stamp Duty' compliance task assigned to HR Manager with due date = hire_date + 25 days (5-day buffer before the 30-day statutory deadline)
Task includes: employee name, hire_date (= contract signing date assumed), stamp duty amount (RM10), and a link to e-Duti Setem on MyTax portal
Employee record gains two fields: custom_stamp_duty_status Select ('Pending' / 'Completed' / 'Overdue') and custom_stamp_duty_ref (Data) for storing the LHDN e-Duti Setem reference number
When custom_stamp_duty_ref is populated, custom_stamp_duty_status auto-updates to 'Completed' and the compliance task is auto-closed
If task is not closed by due date, custom_stamp_duty_status changes to 'Overdue' and an escalation alert fires to Payroll Manager
Employees hired before 2026-01-01 are excluded from stamp duty task creation; a filter is applied on the hire_date
technicalNotes: Stamp Act 1949, First Schedule Item 4: RM10 fixed duty per employment contract
e-Duti Setem portal accessible via MyTax from December 15, 2025; mandatory stamping enforcement from January 1, 2026
30-day deadline runs from the date the contract is executed (signed), which is typically the hire_date or offer acceptance date
For contracts signed before January 1, 2026 but during 2025: no late penalty if stamped by December 31, 2025
LHDN e-Duti Setem announcement: https://stamps.hasil.gov.my/stamps/
The Vibes mandatory stamping announcement: https://www.thevibes.com/articles/news/109052/lhdn-to-enforce-mandatory-stamping-of-employment-contracts-from-2026
dependencies: Track Employment Contract Stamp Duty Compliance via e-Duti Setem MyTax (Mandatory from 2026)
New Hire Statutory Compliance Checklist with Multi-Agency Deadline Tracker

stories.title: HRD Corp RM50,000 Levy Balance Threshold Tracker and 15% Auto-Deduction Ledger Reconciliation
stories.description: Starting March 1, 2025, HRD Corp automatically deducts 15% of an employer's accumulated unused levy balance when two conditions are met: (1) the year-end levy balance exceeds RM50,000 AND (2) the training utilization rate is below the threshold. The auto-deducted amount funds the Latihan Madani programme. A two-month grace period (January–February each year) allows employers to utilize levy before the deduction applies. The existing story 'Track HRD Corp Levy Utilization Rate and Alert on 15% Under-Utilisation Penalty' focuses on a general alert but does not model the RM50,000 threshold condition, the automatic deduction mechanism (distinct from a fine), or the ledger reconciliation entry when HRD Corp actually executes the deduction.
acceptanceCriteria: HRD Corp Levy Ledger shows: monthly levy contributions, approved training grant claims, running balance, and year-to-date utilization rate (%)
Alert Type 1 (Threshold Warning): fires when accumulated year-end levy balance is projected to exceed RM50,000 AND utilization rate < 50%, triggered during November–December
Alert Type 2 (Grace Period Reminder): fires each January 1 if the employer met the deduction trigger conditions in the prior year, reminding HR to utilize levy before February 28

Reconciliation Entry: a HRD Corp Auto-Deduction record can be manually created when HRD Corp executes the 15% deduction, capturing: deduction_date, deduction_amount, hrdcorp_notification_ref, program ('Latihan MADANI'), and reducing the ledger balance accordingly
The levy dashboard shows a 'Deduction Risk' banner when balance > RM50,000 and utilization < 50%
Levy utilization rate is computed as: (sum of approved grant claims for current year) / (sum of levy contributions for current year) × 100
technicalNotes: HRD Corp policy: 15% deduction applies only when levy balance ≥ RM50,000 (for the immediately preceding year's balance) AND utilization is low
This is an automatic deduction BY HRD Corp from the employer's levy account — not an administrative fine — and is used to fund Latihan MADANI
Grace period is January 1 to February 28 annually — employers can avoid deduction by spending down the levy on HRD Corp-approved training before March 1
Voluntary-registered employers (5–9 employees, 0.5% rate) have lower levy accumulation and are less likely to reach RM50,000
OTC Training Centre article on 15% deduction: https://otc.com.my/15-deduction-of-unused-hrd-corp-levy-starting-1-march-2025/
HRD Corp FAQs: https://hrdcorp.gov.my/faq
dependencies: Track HRD Corp Levy Utilization Rate and Alert on 15% Under-Utilisation Penalty
HRD Corp eTRIS Monthly Levy Payment Deadline Alert and Payment Reference Tracking

stories.title: Workplace Harassment Penal Code Amendment — Criminal Offense Escalation Flag on HR Cases (Effective July 2025)
stories.description: Effective July 11, 2025, Malaysia amended the Penal Code to make workplace bullying and harassment a criminal offense. This creates a two-track compliance obligation: (1) the internal investigation track under Employment Act Section 81F (30-day employer investigation, up to RM50,000 administrative penalty) and (2) the criminal escalation track under the Penal Code (police report, potential prosecution). The existing story 'Employment Act Amendment 2025 — Sexual Harassment Mandatory 30-Day Investigation Tracker with RM50,000 Penalty Alert' covers only the Employment Act track. Employers need to distinguish cases where the complainant or HR has escalated to a criminal complaint — different timelines, different actors, and different reporting obligations apply.
acceptanceCriteria: HR Harassment Case DocType gains a custom_case_track Select field: 'Internal Investigation (Employment Act S.81F)' / 'Criminal Complaint (Penal Code S.507A)' / 'Both Tracks'
If 'Criminal Complaint' is selected, a police_report_ref field (Data) appears and the 30-day internal resolution timer is paused (criminal proceedings take precedence)
Criminal track record stores: police_report_date, police_station, case_number, outcome (Ongoing / Charges Filed / Acquitted / Convicted / Withdrawn)
Annual Harassment Compliance Summary distinguishes internal-track vs. criminal-track cases in separate sections
When 'Both Tracks' is selected, both the 30-day internal timer AND the criminal outcome field are active simultaneously

A Legal Notice warning banner appears on the case record when criminal track is selected: 'This case has been escalated to criminal proceedings — engage legal counsel and notify Management immediately'
technicalNotes: Malaysia Penal Code Amendment effective July 11, 2025 — criminalized workplace harassment/bullying as new offense
Anti-Sexual Harassment Act 2022 also in force — independent complaints body separate from employer investigation
Employment Act S.81F: employer must investigate within 30 days; failure = RM50,000 fine
Criminal track: complainant or employer can lodge police report under amended Penal Code; this is independent of the internal investigation
Aliran article on no more silent suffering: https://m.aliran.com/newsletters/no-more-silent-suffering-malaysias-new-laws-tackle-harassment-head-on
Herbert Smith Freehills on Anti-Sexual Harassment Act: https://www.hsfkramer.com/notes/employment/2023-12/malaysia-anti-sexual-harassment-act-2022
dependencies: Employment Act Amendment 2025 — Sexual Harassment Mandatory 30-Day Investigation Tracker with RM50,000 Penalty Alert

stories.title: EIS Amendment 2025 S.45F — Moratorium Phase Suppression and Voluntary Job Vacancy Reporting Module
stories.description: The Employment Insurance System (Amendment) Act 2025 introduces Section 45F requiring employers to notify PERKESO in writing about job vacancies before hiring and confirm fill within 7 days of placement (penalty: RM10,000). However, PERKESO has announced a 2-year moratorium comprising (1) a design-and-consult phase and (2) a voluntary low-risk reporting phase before mandatory enforcement. The existing story 'EIS Amendment 2025 (S.45F) — Employer Job Vacancy Pre-Notification and 7-Day Post-Filling PERKESO Alert' covers the notification mechanism but not the moratorium management. During the moratorium, penalty alerts must be suppressed; employers who choose to voluntarily participate need an opt-in mechanism.
acceptanceCriteria: Company record gains a custom_eis_s45f_mode field: 'Moratorium — Not Reporting' / 'Voluntary Reporting (Early Adopter)' / 'Mandatory Reporting' with an effective_date for mode transitions
All EIS S.45F penalty alerts and notifications are suppressed when mode is 'Moratorium — Not Reporting'
When mode is 'Voluntary Reporting', vacancy notification records can be created and submitted; the system logs voluntary submissions separately for early-adopter audit trail
A system-wide compliance reminder fires 90 days before the moratorium end date (configured in System Settings as custom_eis_moratorium_end_date)
On moratorium expiry, custom_eis_s45f_mode auto-transitions to 'Mandatory Reporting' and S.45F notifications become active
Employer groups back EIS vacancy reporting reforms — moratorium phase shows in compliance calendar as 'Voluntary — No Penalty Until [date]'
technicalNotes: EIS (Amendment) Act 2025, new Section 45F — enacted 2025; penalty RM10,000 for non-compliance after enforcement
PERKESO moratorium: 2 years from Act commencement date — Phase 1: design/consult roundtables; Phase 2: voluntary use
SMEs are the primary target for the moratorium extension (Lexology, NST articles)
The reporting mechanism is to PERKESO ASSIST Portal; API endpoint not yet published
Lexology EIS amendment detail: https://www.lexology.com/library/detail.aspx?g=307fc1fa-bdbb-47aa-8a39-89fe02c562ce
NST employer groups backing reforms: https://www.nst.com.my/amp/business/corporate/2025/12/1334538/employer-groups-back-eis-vacancy-reporting-reforms-urge-practical-implementation
dependencies: EIS Amendment 2025 (S.45F) — Employer Job Vacancy Pre-Notification and 7-Day Post-Filling PERKESO Alert
Track Statutory Compliance Submission Calendar and Auto-Escalate Overdue Filings

stories.title: Progressive Wage Policy — 21-Hour Annual Training Completion Tracking per Eligible Employee
stories.description: Employers participating in Malaysia's Progressive Wage Policy (PWP) must ensure each eligible employee (local, full-time, earning RM1,700–RM4,999/month) completes a minimum of 21 training hours per calendar year as a condition for receiving the government wage subsidy (RM200 million budget, targeting 50,000 workers in 2025–2026). Training hours not completed in 2025 can be carried forward until December 2026. The system must track training hours per employee, link to HRD Corp eTRIS-approved training records, and validate the 21-hour threshold for each eligible employee before the PWP subsidy claim is generated. The existing 'Progressive Wage Policy (PWP) Eligibility Flag and Wage Escalation Compliance Report' story tracks wage levels but does not address the mandatory training hours dimension.
acceptanceCriteria: Employee DocType gains a custom_pwp_eligible Check field (auto-set when nationality='Malaysian', employment_type='Full-Time', and basic_salary between RM1,700 and RM4,999)
A PWP Training Log child table (or linked DocType) records training sessions per employee: date, course_name, provider, hours_completed, hrdcorp_etris_ref, and carry_forward_year flag
System computes per-employee: training_hours_this_year (sum of current-year logs) and carry_forward_hours (from prior year, valid until December 2026)

PWP Training Compliance Report lists all eligible employees with: total hours completed, carry-forward hours applied, shortfall vs. 21-hour target, and PWP subsidy claim readiness (Ready / Shortfall / Not Started)
Alert fires to HR Manager by October 31 each year for employees who have not yet met the 21-hour threshold, giving 2 months to schedule remaining training
PWP subsidy claim generation is blocked for employees with training shortfall; override requires Payroll Manager approval
technicalNotes: PWP launched June 2024; RM200 million budget for 2025; targets local private-sector employees in RM1,700–RM4,999 wage band
21-hour training requirement per employee per year; 2025 uncompleted hours carry forward to December 2026
Training providers must be HRD Corp or JTK-approved; government-linked companies and MNCs are excluded from the subsidy scheme
PWP managed by SOCSO; subsidy application via SOCSO portal — not through LHDN
Omni HR PWP guide: https://www.omnihr.co/blog/progressive-wage-model-malaysia
Malaysia Progressive Wage Policy ISIS analysis: https://www.isis.org.my/2024/03/25/malaysias-progressive-wage-policy/
dependencies: Progressive Wage Policy (PWP) Eligibility Flag and Wage Escalation Compliance Report
HRD Corp eTRIS Training Claim Recording and Levy Utilization Dashboard

stories.title: Gig Workers Bill 2025 — Platform Provider Registration as PERKESO Contributing Entity with Mandatory 1.25% LINDUNG KENDIRI Deduction on Royal Assent
stories.description: The Malaysia Gig Workers Bill 2025, passed by the Senate on September 9, 2025 and awaiting royal assent, mandates platform providers (digital intermediary system operators) to register with PERKESO as a 'Contributing Entity' and integrate via API for automatic deduction of 1.25% of each gig worker's earnings for LINDUNG KENDIRI (formerly SKSPS). Unlike voluntary SKSPS contributions, this is a mandatory statutory obligation triggered on royal assent. The existing gig worker stories reference voluntary SKSPS registration; this story covers the mandatory Contributing Entity onboarding, the API integration for per-transaction contribution calculation, and the monthly aggregate remittance to PERKESO on behalf of all platform gig workers.
acceptanceCriteria: Company DocType gains a custom_is_gig_platform (Check) flag; when checked, a 'Gig Platform Contributing Entity' section appears with: custom_perkeso_contributing_entity_id (Data), custom_perkeso_api_credential_ref (Data), and custom_gig_platform_api_integrated (Check)
Platform-type employers can create Gig Worker records (separate from Employee) with fields: worker_name, nric_or_passport, perkeso_member_id, platform_join_date, contribution_rate (default 1.25%)
Per-transaction earnings recorded against each Gig Worker; monthly aggregate contribution = sum of all transaction earnings × 1.25% for each gig worker
Monthly LINDUNG KENDIRI remittance report generates a per-worker contribution summary file for PERKESO ASSIST Portal submission
System validates that each active Gig Worker has a valid PERKESO LINDUNG KENDIRI registration before allowing monthly remittance
A 'Contributing Entity Registration' checklist task is auto-created on royal assent date (configurably set) prompting HR to register via PERKESO ASSIST Portal
technicalNotes: Gig Workers Bill 2025 passed Senate September 9, 2025; awaiting royal assent — implementation date = royal assent date
Contribution rate: 1.25% of gig worker earnings deducted from gig worker's payment and remitted to PERKESO LINDUNG KENDIRI
Platform providers must integrate via PERKESO API (spec not yet published); manual upload via ASSIST Portal as interim measure
Note: the Bill does NOT require platform providers to contribute employer-side EPF (unlike traditional employers) — only PERKESO deduction from gig earnings
Herbert Smith Freehills Gig Workers Bill analysis: https://www.hsfkramer.com/notes/employment/2025-posts/malaysia-gig-workers-bill-2025
DFDL Gig Workers Bill tabling: https://www.dfdl.com/insights/legal-and-tax-updates/malaysia-gig-workers-bill-2025-tabled-for-first-reading/
dependencies: Gig Workers Act 2025 — Auto-Register Gig Workers Under PERKESO SKSPS via ASSIST Portal API
Gig Workers Act 2025 — SKSPS Per-Transaction Contribution Calculation and PERKESO Monthly Aggregate Remittance
PERKESO LINDUNG Scheme Rebranding — Update All System Labels from SKSPS/SKSSR to LINDUNG Family Names

stories.title: VSS/MSS Employer EIS Loss-of-Employment Certification for Retrenched Employee EIS Benefit Claims
stories.description: When an employer conducts a Voluntary Separation Scheme (VSS) or Mutual Separation Scheme (MSS) exercise, each affected employee is eligible for EIS (LINDUNG KERJAYA) job loss benefits including Job Search Allowance (JSA) for 3–6 months. For the employee to claim EIS benefits, the employer must provide a termination letter specifying the correct EIS Loss-of-Employment reason code ('VSS' or 'MSS' or 'Retrenchment') and notify PERKESO ASSIST Portal. If the employer fails to provide the correct documentation within 60 days, the employee cannot access EIS benefits. No existing story covers the VSS/MSS-specific employer obligations for EIS benefit facilitation; the current EIS stories cover general contributions and enhanced benefits display.
acceptanceCriteria: An 'Employee Termination' DocType (or workflow step on Employee record) captures termination_reason Select field including: 'VSS — Voluntary Separation', 'MSS — Mutual Separation', 'Retrenchment', 'Contract End', 'Dismissal', 'Resignation', 'Retirement'

When termination_reason is VSS, MSS, or Retrenchment, system auto-generates an EIS Termination Certificate for the employee containing: employer name, EPF/SOCSO number, employee IC, last working day, EIS reason code, and HR Manager signature line
Certificate template uses PERKESO-required format; includes a QR code link to PERKESO EIS claim page
System creates a 60-day task: 'Issue EIS Termination Certificate to Employee' — if not closed within 60 days, escalation alert fires
Bulk VSS Exercise support: HR can select multiple employees, apply the same termination_reason, and bulk-generate EIS Termination Certificates in a single action
A VSS/MSS Exercise record aggregates: exercise_name, start_date, affected_count, certificates_issued, certificates_pending
technicalNotes: EIS Act 2017 (LINDUNG KERJAYA): employees must apply for JSA within 60 days of Loss of Employment date
EIS Loss-of-Employment codes accepted by PERKESO: VSS, MSS, Retrenchment, Constructive Dismissal, Natural Disaster, Company Closure
Employer's primary obligation: provide clear termination letter with reason and SOCSO number; no API submission required — employee self-applies via MySOCSOPayroll portal
JSA benefit: 80% of wage for month 1, tapering to 50% by month 6; capped at EIS contribution ceiling (RM6,000)
PERKESO EIS official: https://www.perkeso.gov.my/en/benefits/employment-insurance-system.html
Malaysia.gov.my EIS guide: https://www.malaysia.gov.my/en/categories/aid-welfare-and-assistance/job-aid/employment-insurance-scheme-eis-assistance-under-perkeso
dependencies: EIS Amendment 2025 — Display Updated Statutory EIS Benefit Rates on Employee Termination Workflow
Implement Termination and Lay-Off Benefits Calculator

stories.title: PDPA 2024 Payroll Data Retention Schedule — 7-Year LHDN Override vs. PDPA Data Minimization Conflict Resolution
stories.description: The Personal Data Protection (Amendment) Act 2024 (effective June 2025) requires organisations to delete personal data when the purpose of processing is no longer needed (data minimization principle). However, ITA Section 82 mandates that employers retain payroll tax records for 7 years, and the Employment Act requires 6-year payroll wage register retention. These legal obligations are in direct conflict for ex-employee personal data: PDPA says delete when purpose expires, while LHDN and MOHR say retain for 6–7 years. The payroll system must explicitly model a 'Statutory Retention Lock' that prevents PDPA-driven data deletion for records within the mandatory retention window, and auto-releases the lock when the retention period expires.
acceptanceCriteria: Employee record (and linked Salary Slips, EA Forms, EPF/SOCSO submissions) gains a custom_statutory_retention_until (Date) field computed as max(termination_date + 7 years [LHDN ITA S.82], termination_date + 6 years [EA wage register])

When custom_statutory_retention_until is in the future, a 'Statutory Retention Lock' banner appears on the Employee record preventing deletion and displaying the reason ('LHDN ITA S.82' or 'Employment Act S.61A')
System prevents any bulk-delete or anonymization action on locked employee records; delete action requires override by System Manager with documented justification
A Data Retention Dashboard lists all ex-employees with: termination date, retention_until date, retention_status (Locked / Unlocked), and days until lock expires
When custom_statutory_retention_until date passes, system sends a notification to Data Protection Officer (DPO) that retention obligation has expired and PDPA data minimization can be applied
DPO Compliance Log records: notification_sent_date, dpo_action (Anonymized / Deleted / Retained with justification), action_date, and reason_note
technicalNotes: PDPA 2024 Amendment: data minimization principle effective June 2025; DPO appointment mandatory for large-scale processors
ITA 1967 Section 82: employer must retain tax records (payroll, PCB, EA forms, CP39) for 7 years from the year of assessment
Employment Act 1955 implied through court precedent: payroll wage register retained 6 years (also covered by 'Implement Payroll Wage Register with 6-Year Retention' story)
The 7-year LHDN retention is the longer obligation and takes precedence — use max(6, 7) = 7 years as the retention lock period
PDPA 2024 Amendment Act A1727: https://www.pdp.gov.my/ppdpv1/en/akta/personal-data-protection-amendment-act-2024/
ASEAN Briefing PDPA enforcement from June 2025: https://www.aseanbriefing.com/news/malaysia-tightens-data-protection-from-june-2025/
dependencies: Implement Payroll Wage Register with 6-Year Retention and LHDN/MOHR Audit Export
Enforce ITA Section 82 7-Year Payroll Tax Record Retention (Separate from MOHR 6-Year)
PDPA 2024 DPO Appointment Registry and 72-Hour Payroll Data Breach Notification Workflow
